one_time_description: Insurance proceeds received during the year, non-recurring item
economic_notes: First Nation receives share of Ontario provincial gross gaming revenues (1.7% of aggregate provincial gross gaming revenues) through Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership, restricted to community development, health, education, cultural and economic development. 69% of revenue from ISC (down from 72% prior year). Investment in Chi Mino Ozhitoowin LP increased significantly to $38,371.
notes: Audit is qualified due to unaudited limited partnership statements for the Ontario First Nations LP investment — earnings cannot be verified and adjustment may be necessary to surplus and accumulated surplus for both 2024 and 2023. Prior year (2023) was also audited by a different firm and carried a qualified opinion. Repayable funding of $887,592 represents amounts owed back to ISC and Ministry of Health. Unexpended funding of $6,919,585 is large relative to revenue, particularly in Education Services ($4,429,150) and Child and Family Services ($919,363), suggesting significant unspent restricted funding carried forward. Seven government business partnerships accounted for by modified equity method. No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC ($2,987,136) + FNIHB ($536,221) + Provincial ($101,400) = $3,624,757; osr calculated as total revenue before transfers adjustments ($3,189,894) minus government transfers recognized ($3,624,757) would be negative — instead osr reflects Other revenue category total of $1,470,204 per statement structure where net transfer adjustments reduce total revenue.
